title: The Scholar's Trial
storySnippet: Lira faces the Scholar's Trial, where only mastery of the most powerful words will see her through.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Seeing a knight use a laptop in the medieval play was clearly ___.
data.definition: Anachronistic: belonging to a different time period; noticeably out of place in the era depicted.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The idea of wasting resources is ___ to the goals of the school's conservation club.
data.definition: Antithetical: directly opposed or contrasted; completely opposite in nature.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: A rainbow appearing on the morning of the championship felt like an ___ sign for the team.
data.definition: Auspicious: indicating a good chance of success; favorable and promising.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Instead of giving a straight answer, the witness used ___ to avoid the question.
data.definition: Circumlocution: the use of many words where fewer would do, especially to avoid saying something directly.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Pollution can have ___ effects on wildlife habitats for many years.
data.definition: Deleterious: causing harm or damage; injurious to health or well-being.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ nature of the documentary made it feel like a classroom lesson about ocean life.
data.definition: Didactic: intended to teach or instruct, sometimes in a way that feels overly moralistic.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The long hike in the summer heat began to ___ even the strongest members of the group.
data.definition: Enervate: to drain of energy or vitality; to weaken physically or mentally.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ professor could speak knowledgeably about subjects ranging from ancient Rome to quantum physics.
data.definition: Erudite: having or showing great knowledge gained from study and reading; deeply learned.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Skipping warm-ups before practice will only ___ the risk of getting injured.
data.definition: Exacerbate: to make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ artist challenged traditional painting styles by using unusual materials like sand and wire.
data.definition: Iconoclastic: challenging or rejecting widely accepted beliefs, traditions, or institutions.
